text: In both business and daily life, risk management is a vital skill. The first step involves identifying potential risks, such as financial losses or project delays. Once these risks are recognized, you can develop mitigation strategies to reduce their impact, like diversifying investments or improving safety procedures. It is also important to have contingency plans in place—these are backup actions you take if a risk actually occurs, for example, having extra supplies or alternative solutions. Finally, continuous monitoring allows you to track changes and adjust your plans as needed. By following this structured approach, you can handle uncertainties more confidently and minimize negative outcomes.
